name: Interview — Music Bed
summary: Sparse, slow interview held together by a continuous music bed. Few cuts, long held reframes — feels editorial and composed rather than chopped.
best_for: Brand/founder films, reflective interviews, documentary-leaning pieces.

pacing.note: Let shots breathe; change on musical phrase boundaries and B-roll opportunities.

transitions.philosophy: Choose the transition that fits the moment; never default to a hard cut.
transitions.notes: Dissolve to B-roll and back on phrase boundaries; avoid abrupt changes.

audio.note: Music sidechain-ducks under speech; swells in B-roll gaps.

generator_rules: Lay continuous music bed; duck under dialogue.
Cut on musical phrases and to B-roll, not on every pause.
Use slow push-ins to keep static shots alive.
